metadata.title: Observability Full Coverage — Wire ALL Flows, ALL Containers, ALL Hooks
metadata.priority: P0 — blocks next factorial pass (won't launch until every flow is observable)

phase.title: Observability Helpers — Reusable Flow Entry/Exit Wrappers
phase.description:
Before wiring observability into 15 flow files, create reusable helper functions that encapsulate the observability contract. This avoids copy-pasting 10+ lines of boilerplate into every flow.
Two helpers: (a) flow_observability_context() — a context manager that handles: - configure_flow_logging(logs_dir) - StructuredLogger.log_flow_start() - yield (flow body executes) - StructuredLogger.log_flow_end(status, duration_ms) - Exception handling: log_flow_end(status="failed") on error
(b) gpu_flow_observability_context() — extends (a) with: - require_cuda_context(flow_name) - GpuHeartbeatMonitor(output_dir=logs_dir) - Both wrapped as context managers
This is the "library-first" approach to observability wiring — each flow file calls one function instead of importing 5 modules.

task.title: Create src/minivess/observability/flow_observability.py
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test flow_observability_context(): (a) Creates StructuredLogger with the given flow_name (b) Calls configure_flow_logging() with the given logs_dir (c) Emits flow_start event on __enter__ (d) Emits flow_end event with status="completed" and duration_ms on __exit__ (e) On exception: emits flow_end with status="failed", then re-raises (f) Returns the StructuredLogger instance for additional task-level logging (g) Reads LOGS_DIR from os.environ if logs_dir=None (with loud failure if unset)
Test gpu_flow_observability_context(): (a) Calls require_cuda_context(flow_name) before entering (b) Wraps body in GpuHeartbeatMonitor(output_dir=logs_dir) (c) All flow_observability_context() behavior plus GPU-specific guards (d) Heartbeat monitor is started on __enter__, stopped on __exit__ (e) MINIVESS_ALLOW_CPU=1 bypasses CUDA guard (test escape hatch)
Test with monkeypatch for env vars, mock for CUDA availability.

phase.success-criteria: - flow_observability_context() emits flow_start + flow_end events - gpu_flow_observability_context() additionally calls CUDA guard + heartbeat - Both are context managers compatible with `with` statement - LOGS_DIR env var read with loud failure (Rule #25) - All tests in make test-staging (mocked CUDA, no GPU required)

phase.title: Wire CUDA Guard into ALL 4 GPU Flows
phase.description:
Currently require_cuda_context() is ONLY called in train_flow.py (line 1465). The other 3 GPU flows (hpo, post_training, analysis) call require_docker_context() but NOT require_cuda_context(). This means they would ALSO silently fall back to CPU if CUDA becomes unavailable.
This phase adds require_cuda_context() to ALL 4 GPU flows, immediately after the existing require_docker_context() call.
GPU flow identification (from docker-compose.flows.yml — services with `devices: nvidia.com/gpu=all`): - train: train_flow.py line 1461 (docker), 1465 (cuda) — ALREADY DONE - hpo: hpo_flow.py line 165 (docker) — NEEDS CUDA GUARD - post_training: post_training_flow.py line 261 (docker) — NEEDS CUDA GUARD - analyze: analysis_flow.py line 1784 (docker) — NEEDS CUDA GUARD
NOTE: hpo_flow.py currently does NOT import cuda_guard. The HPO orchestrator itself may run on CPU (it delegates training to run_deployment()), but when used in HYBRID mode (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES per replica), it needs GPU access. We guard it anyway — fail-fast is always better than silent fallback.

task.title: Add require_cuda_context() to hpo_flow.py
task.tdd-spec: RED: AST test verifying hpo_flow.py: (a) Imports require_cuda_context from minivess.orchestration.cuda_guard (b) Calls require_cuda_context("hpo") in the hpo_flow() function body GREEN: Add import + call after require_docker_context("hpo") at line 165.

task.title: Add require_cuda_context() to post_training_flow.py
task.tdd-spec: RED: AST test verifying post_training_flow.py: (a) Imports require_cuda_context (b) Calls require_cuda_context("post-training") in post_training_flow() body GREEN: Add import + call after require_docker_context("post-training") at line 261.

task.title: Add require_cuda_context() to analysis_flow.py
task.tdd-spec: RED: AST test verifying analysis_flow.py: (a) Imports require_cuda_context (b) Calls require_cuda_context("analysis") in run_analysis_flow() body GREEN: Add import + call after require_docker_context("analysis") at line 1784.

task.title: Create comprehensive AST enforcement test for ALL GPU flows
task.tdd-spec:
RED: A SINGLE test file that parametrizes over ALL 4 GPU flow files and verifies via AST inspection: (a) Each file imports require_cuda_context from cuda_guard (b) Each file calls require_cuda_context() inside the @flow-decorated function (c) The call occurs BEFORE any model creation, data loading, or heavy computation
The test uses ast.parse() + ast.walk() to find ImportFrom and Call nodes. The GPU flow list is: [train_flow.py, hpo_flow.py, post_training_flow.py, analysis_flow.py]
IMPORTANT: This replaces the narrow test_train_flow_cuda_guard.py with a comprehensive all-flows version. The old file can be deleted or kept as a historical artifact.

phase.success-criteria: - ALL 4 GPU flows import and call require_cuda_context() - AST test parametrized over all 4 files — adding a new GPU flow without the guard will FAIL the test - MINIVESS_ALLOW_CPU=1 bypasses in all 4 flows (test escape hatch) - All tests in make test-staging

phase.title: Wire GpuHeartbeatMonitor into ALL 4 GPU Flows
phase.description:
GpuHeartbeatMonitor exists but is NOT wired into ANY flow. This phase wraps the compute-intensive loop in each GPU flow with the heartbeat context manager.
Where to place the heartbeat in each flow: - train_flow.py: Wrap the fold iteration loop (training_subflow) - hpo_flow.py: Wrap the study.optimize() call - post_training_flow.py: Wrap the plugin execution section - analysis_flow.py: Wrap the evaluation + ensemble section
The heartbeat monitor: - Writes heartbeat.json to LOGS_DIR every 30s - Logs ERROR if GPU util < 5% for > 120s - Enables Docker healthcheck to detect stuck containers

task.title: Wire GpuHeartbeatMonitor into train_flow.py
task.tdd-spec:
RED: AST test verifying train_flow.py: (a) Imports GpuHeartbeatMonitor from observability.gpu_heartbeat (b) Uses it as a context manager (`with GpuHeartbeatMonitor(...)`) somewhere in the training flow or subflow function body
Functional test: (c) In a unit test with mocked GpuHeartbeatMonitor, verify __enter__ and __exit__ are called when the training subflow executes

task.title: Wire GpuHeartbeatMonitor into hpo_flow.py
task.tdd-spec: RED: AST test verifying hpo_flow.py imports GpuHeartbeatMonitor and uses it as context manager around study.optimize().

task.title: Wire GpuHeartbeatMonitor into post_training_flow.py
task.tdd-spec: RED: AST test verifying post_training_flow.py imports GpuHeartbeatMonitor and uses it as context manager around the plugin execution loop.

task.title: Wire GpuHeartbeatMonitor into analysis_flow.py
task.tdd-spec: RED: AST test verifying analysis_flow.py imports GpuHeartbeatMonitor and uses it as context manager around the evaluation section.

task.title: Create comprehensive AST enforcement test for GPU heartbeat
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Parametrized test over all 4 GPU flow files verifying: (a) Each file imports GpuHeartbeatMonitor (b) Each file contains a `with` statement using GpuHeartbeatMonitor (c) Reads output_dir from LOGS_DIR env var
Uses ast.parse() to find ImportFrom nodes and With nodes containing GpuHeartbeatMonitor in their context_expr.

phase.success-criteria: - ALL 4 GPU flows import and use GpuHeartbeatMonitor as context manager - heartbeat.json written to LOGS_DIR during GPU flow execution - AST enforcement test catches any GPU flow without heartbeat - Monitor is daemon thread — does not prevent container exit - All tests in make test-staging (mocked pynvml)

phase.title: Wire Structured Logging + JSONL into ALL 15 Flows
phase.description:
Currently StructuredLogger and configure_flow_logging() are only used in hpo_flow.py. This phase wires them into ALL 15 flow files so that every flow emits: - flow_start event at entry - flow_end event at exit (with status + duration) - Durable JSONL file at LOGS_DIR/{flow_name}.jsonl
Implementation approach: Use the flow_observability_context() helper from Phase 1 (or inline the 3 calls if the helper is not yet available). Each flow file gets:
1. `from minivess.observability.flow_observability import flow_observability_context` 2. At the top of the @flow function body, after docker/cuda guards: ```python with flow_observability_context("flow-name") as slog: # ... existing flow body ... ```
For GPU flows, use gpu_flow_observability_context() which additionally wraps CUDA guard + heartbeat monitor. This means Phases 2 + 3 wiring can optionally be consolidated here — but explicit is better than implicit, so we keep the separate CUDA guard calls and add the context manager wrapper.
The 15 flow files with their @flow entry functions: GPU: training_flow, hpo_flow, post_training_flow, run_analysis_flow CPU: data_flow, biostatistics_flow, acquisition_flow, deploy_flow, dashboard_flow, annotation_flow, pipeline_flow, qa_flow, drift_simulation_flow, synthetic_generation_flow, maintenance_flow

task.title: Wire structured logging into all GPU flows (4 files)
task.tdd-spec:
RED: For each GPU flow file, AST test verifying: (a) Imports StructuredLogger from observability.structured_logging OR imports flow_observability_context from observability.flow_observability (b) Creates a StructuredLogger or flow_observability_context with the flow name (c) Calls log_flow_start() (directly or via context manager)
Also verify configure_flow_logging() is called in each GPU flow (currently only in hpo_flow.py).

task.title: Wire structured logging into all CPU flows (11 files)
task.tdd-spec:
RED: For each CPU flow file, AST test verifying: (a) Imports StructuredLogger or flow_observability_context (b) Creates a StructuredLogger with the flow name (c) Calls log_flow_start() (directly or via context manager) (d) Calls configure_flow_logging() for durable JSONL output
The 11 CPU flow files: data_flow.py, biostatistics_flow.py, acquisition_flow.py, deploy_flow.py, dashboard_flow.py, annotation_flow.py, pipeline_flow.py, qa_flow.py, drift_simulation_flow.py, synthetic_generation_flow.py, maintenance_flow.py
task.deliverable: All 11 CPU flow files (modified — ~5-8 lines each)

task.title: Create comprehensive AST enforcement test for ALL flows
task.tdd-spec:
RED: A SINGLE test file that parametrizes over ALL 15 flow files and verifies: (a) Each file imports StructuredLogger OR flow_observability_context (b) Each file calls configure_flow_logging() for durable JSONL
The flow file list is maintained as a constant in the test, matching the actual files in src/minivess/orchestration/flows/.
ADDITIONALLY: A discovery test that scans the flows/ directory for *_flow.py files and verifies that every discovered file is in the parametrized list. This catches new flow files added without observability wiring.

phase.success-criteria: - ALL 15 flow files emit flow_start/flow_end structured events - ALL 15 flow files write durable JSONL logs via configure_flow_logging() - Discovery test catches any new flow file added without observability - LOGS_DIR/{flow_name}.jsonl created for every flow - All tests in make test-staging

phase.title: Apply Prefect Task Timing Hooks to ALL 76 @task Decorators
phase.description:
create_task_timing_hooks() exists in prefect_hooks.py but is NOT applied to ANY @task decorator in any flow file. This phase applies the hooks to ALL 76 @task decorators across 15 flow files.
Implementation approach: Each flow file gets these additions at the module level: ```python from minivess.observability.prefect_hooks import create_task_timing_hooks _on_complete, _on_fail = create_task_timing_hooks() ```
Then every @task decorator changes from: ```python @task(name="task-name") ``` to: ```python @task(name="task-name", on_completion=[_on_complete], on_failure=[_on_fail]) ```
This provides: - Task name + duration logged on completion - Task name + duration + error type logged on failure - All visible in Prefect UI + Docker logs + JSONL
BREAKDOWN by file (76 total @task decorators): data_flow.py: 15 tasks biostatistics_flow.py: 15 tasks analysis_flow.py: 14 tasks acquisition_flow.py: 6 tasks deploy_flow.py: 5 tasks dashboard_flow.py: 5 tasks train_flow.py: 4 tasks qa_flow.py: 3 tasks drift_simulation_flow.py: 3 tasks annotation_flow.py: 2 tasks synthetic_generation_flow.py: 1 task post_training_flow.py: 1 task pipeline_flow.py: 1 task maintenance_flow.py: 1 task hpo_flow.py: 1 task

task.title: Apply hooks to high-task-count flows (data, biostat, analysis)
task.tdd-spec:
RED: AST test verifying that every @task decorator in data_flow.py, biostatistics_flow.py, and analysis_flow.py includes on_completion and on_failure keyword arguments.
AST inspection strategy: 1. Parse file with ast.parse() 2. Walk the AST looking for FunctionDef nodes decorated with @task 3. For each @task Call node, verify it has keyword arguments named "on_completion" and "on_failure" 4. Verify the keyword values reference _on_complete and _on_fail
GREEN: Add the import + variable definition at module level, then modify each @task decorator to include the hooks.

task.title: Apply hooks to medium-task-count flows (acq, deploy, dash, train, qa, drift)
task.tdd-spec: RED: Same AST test pattern as 5.1, parametrized for: acquisition_flow.py (6), deploy_flow.py (5), dashboard_flow.py (5), train_flow.py (4), qa_flow.py (3), drift_simulation_flow.py (3)

task.title: Apply hooks to low-task-count flows (annotation, synthetic, post_training, pipeline, maintenance, hpo)
task.tdd-spec: RED: Same AST test pattern for remaining 6 files with 1-2 tasks each: annotation_flow.py (2), synthetic_generation_flow.py (1), post_training_flow.py (1), pipeline_flow.py (1), maintenance_flow.py (1), hpo_flow.py (1)

task.title: Create comprehensive AST enforcement test for ALL @task hooks
task.tdd-spec:
RED: A SINGLE test file that: 1. Discovers all *_flow.py files in src/minivess/orchestration/flows/ 2. Parses each with ast.parse() 3. Finds ALL @task-decorated functions 4. Verifies EVERY @task has on_completion and on_failure kwargs 5. Reports WHICH tasks are missing hooks (for easy fixing)
Additionally: 6. Counts total @task decorators found and asserts >= 76 (catches accidental removal of @task decorators) 7. Verifies each file imports create_task_timing_hooks

phase.success-criteria: - ALL 76 @task decorators have on_completion + on_failure hooks - AST enforcement test catches any new @task added without hooks - Total task count assertion prevents accidental decorator removal - Task timing visible in Prefect UI for ALL tasks - All tests in make test-staging

phase.title: CPU Flow Healthcheck Script
phase.description:
The existing healthcheck_training.py checks heartbeat.json staleness — this is appropriate for GPU flows that write heartbeat via GpuHeartbeatMonitor. CPU flows do NOT have a heartbeat monitor (they don't use GPU), so they need a different health check strategy.
CPU flow health check strategy: 1. Check if the flow's JSONL log file exists (written by configure_flow_logging) 2. Check if the JSONL file has been updated within the last N minutes 3. Check if the main flow process is alive (PID 1 in container)
This is simpler than the GPU heartbeat check but still catches: - Flow that crashed silently - Flow that hung without producing output - Container alive but flow process dead
The script uses ONLY stdlib (same constraint as healthcheck_training.py).

task.title: Create scripts/healthcheck_cpu_flow.py
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test the health check script that: (a) Reads LOGS_DIR and FLOW_NAME from environment variables (b) Checks if LOGS_DIR/{FLOW_NAME}.jsonl exists (c) Returns exit 0 if the JSONL file was modified in the last STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES (default 30) (d) Returns exit 1 if the JSONL file is stale or missing (e) Returns exit 0 during grace period (HEALTH_GRACE_PERIOD_MINUTES, default 10) (f) Uses ONLY stdlib — no minivess package imports (g) Handles edge case: flow just started, JSONL not yet created
Test with tmp_path for LOGS_DIR, monkeypatch for env vars.

task.title: Verify both healthcheck scripts use only stdlib
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test that both healthcheck_training.py and healthcheck_cpu_flow.py: (a) Do NOT import anything from the minivess package (b) Do NOT import any third-party package (only stdlib modules) (c) AST inspection: scan all ImportFrom and Import nodes, verify each module is in a known stdlib set
This ensures the scripts work in ANY container, regardless of which tier's base image it uses.

phase.success-criteria: - healthcheck_cpu_flow.py works for all CPU flow containers - Uses JSONL file staleness as health signal (not heartbeat.json) - Respects grace period for slow-starting flows - Uses only stdlib — works in any container tier - All tests in make test-staging

phase.title: Apply Docker HEALTHCHECK to ALL Flow Services in Compose
phase.description:
The first pass DOCUMENTED healthcheck stanzas (docs/planning/docker-healthcheck-stanzas.md) but did NOT apply them to docker-compose.flows.yml. This phase applies health checks to ALL flow services — both GPU (using healthcheck_training.py) and CPU (using healthcheck_cpu_flow.py).
The user has authorized compose YAML changes by requesting "full coverage."
GPU services (use heartbeat-based healthcheck): train, hpo, hpo-worker, post_training, analyze
CPU services (use JSONL-based healthcheck): acquisition, data, deploy, dashboard, biostatistics, annotation, pipeline
Non-flow services (no healthcheck — they are static servers): dashboard-api, dashboard-ui, monai-label, gpu-benchmark
For healthcheck to work, the scripts must be COPYed into the Docker images. Current Dockerfiles already COPY scripts/ (verified in first pass), but we need to verify this for ALL flow Dockerfiles.
NOTE: We add healthcheck stanza AND the required env vars (STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES, HEALTH_GRACE_PERIOD_MINUTES) to x-common-env.

task.title: Add healthcheck to GPU services in docker-compose.flows.yml
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test (YAML parsing, NOT regex) that docker-compose.flows.yml: (a) train service has healthcheck with test containing "healthcheck_training.py" (b) hpo service has healthcheck with test containing "healthcheck_training.py" (c) post_training service has healthcheck with test containing "healthcheck_training.py" (d) analyze service has healthcheck with test containing "healthcheck_training.py" (e) All healthchecks have interval, timeout, retries, start_period (f) start_period >= 300s (5 min minimum for GPU flow startup)
GREEN: Add healthcheck stanzas to each GPU service definition. Use the stanzas from docs/planning/docker-healthcheck-stanzas.md as reference.

task.title: Add healthcheck to CPU services in docker-compose.flows.yml
task.tdd-spec: RED: Test (YAML parsing) that docker-compose.flows.yml: (a) acquisition service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (b) data service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (c) deploy service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (d) dashboard service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (e) biostatistics service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (f) annotation service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (g) pipeline service has healthcheck with "healthcheck_cpu_flow.py" (h) All with start_period >= 120s (2 min for CPU flow startup)

task.title: Add healthcheck env vars to x-common-env
Verify healthcheck scripts are COPYed in ALL flow Dockerfiles
task.tdd-spec:
RED: For each Dockerfile in deployment/docker/ that corresponds to a flow service (Dockerfile.train, Dockerfile.hpo, etc.), verify via file content inspection (NOT regex — read + str.partition/split) that: (a) The Dockerfile has a COPY instruction that includes the scripts/ directory (b) The scripts directory is available at runtime (not only in builder stage)
Use Path.read_text() + line iteration + str.startswith("COPY").

phase.success-criteria: - ALL GPU flow services have heartbeat-based Docker HEALTHCHECK - ALL CPU flow services have JSONL-based Docker HEALTHCHECK - docker ps shows health status for all flow containers - Stuck containers detected within stall_threshold + retries interval - .env.example updated with healthcheck configuration variables - All tests in make test-staging

phase.title: .env.example — All Observability Variables in One Batch
phase.description:
Per Rule #22 (Single-Source Config via .env.example), ALL configurable observability values must be in .env.example FIRST. This phase adds a dedicated "Observability" section to .env.example with all variables from the first and second pass.
New variables to add (if not already present): GPU_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_S=30 GPU_HEARTBEAT_LOW_UTIL_THRESHOLD_PCT=5 GPU_HEARTBEAT_ALERT_AFTER_S=120 STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES=30 HEALTH_GRACE_PERIOD_MINUTES=10 OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://minivess-grafana-lgtm:4318
Variables already present (verify, do not duplicate): GRAFANA_PORT=3000 GRAFANA_USER=admin GRAFANA_PASSWORD=admin PROMETHEUS_PORT=9090 OTEL_GRPC_PORT=4317 OTEL_HTTP_PORT=4318

task.title: Add observability section to .env.example
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test that .env.example contains ALL observability variables: - GPU_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_S - GPU_HEARTBEAT_LOW_UTIL_THRESHOLD_PCT - GPU_HEARTBEAT_ALERT_AFTER_S - STALL_THRESHOLD_MINUTES - HEALTH_GRACE_PERIOD_MINUTES - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT
Use the existing test_env_single_source.py pattern: read the file, parse variable names (str.split("=")[0]), verify expected set is present.
GREEN: Append the variables to .env.example under a new section header.
task.test-file: tests/v2/unit/test_env_single_source.py (extended — add new vars to expected set)

task.title: Add heartbeat env vars to x-common-env in compose

phase.success-criteria: - ALL observability env vars in .env.example - ALL env vars referenced in docker-compose.flows.yml x-common-env - No hardcoded values in Python code (functions read from env or config) - test_env_single_source.py catches any missing variable

phase.title: Apply Grafana LGTM + OTEL Stanzas to Docker Compose
phase.description:
The first pass created documentation for the Grafana LGTM stack (docs/planning/grafana-lgtm-compose-stanza.md) and Prefect OTEL config (docs/planning/prefect-otel-config.md). This phase applies these stanzas to the actual docker-compose.yml infrastructure file.
Changes to docker-compose.yml (infra stack): 1. Add grafana-lgtm service (Grafana + OTel Collector + Prometheus + Tempo + Loki) 2. Add OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to the environment for flow containers
Changes to docker-compose.flows.yml: 1. Add OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to x-common-env
This enables: - Prefect traces exported to Grafana via OTLP - Training metrics visible in Grafana dashboards - Log aggregation in Loki - Trace visualization in Tempo
NOTE: grafana-lgtm is an OPTIONAL profile (--profile observability) so it does not add startup overhead for basic development.

task.title: Add grafana-lgtm service to docker-compose.yml
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test (YAML parsing) that docker-compose.yml contains a service named "grafana-lgtm" with: (a) image: grafana/otel-lgtm:latest (b) container_name containing "grafana-lgtm" or "otel-lgtm" (c) ports including 4317 (OTLP gRPC) and 4318 (OTLP HTTP) (d) networks including "minivess" (e) profiles including "observability" (optional activation)
GREEN: Add the service from grafana-lgtm-compose-stanza.md with the observability profile gate.

task.title: Add OTEL endpoint to docker-compose.flows.yml x-common-env
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test (YAML parsing) that docker-compose.flows.yml x-common-env contains OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT with a sensible default pointing to the grafana-lgtm container.
GREEN: Add the variable to x-common-env.

phase.success-criteria: - grafana-lgtm service available behind --profile observability - OTEL endpoint configured in x-common-env for all flow containers - Does NOT increase default startup time (profile-gated) - All tests in make test-staging

phase.title: Wire MLflow Stall Detection into GpuHeartbeatMonitor
phase.description:
stall_detection.py (detect_mlflow_metric_stall) exists but is NOT wired into the heartbeat monitor. This phase connects them so that the heartbeat monitor periodically checks for MLflow metric staleness IN ADDITION to GPU utilization checks.
This catches the scenario where: - Container is healthy (heartbeat updating) - GPU is utilized (model loaded, gradients computed) - BUT no new metrics are being logged to MLflow (logging bug, MLflow down)
The check is OPTIONAL — if MLflow is not available or no run_id is set, the stall check is silently skipped (graceful degradation).

task.title: Add MLflow stall check to GpuHeartbeatMonitor
task.tdd-spec:
RED: Test that GpuHeartbeatMonitor: (a) Accepts an optional mlflow_run_id parameter (b) When mlflow_run_id is set, calls detect_mlflow_metric_stall() periodically (every N heartbeat cycles, not every cycle — avoid MLflow spam) (c) When stall detected, logs ERROR with stall result details (d) When mlflow_run_id is None, skips the check silently (e) When MLflow is unavailable (ImportError), skips gracefully
Test with mocked detect_mlflow_metric_stall() returning various StallResults.

phase.success-criteria: - Heartbeat monitor checks MLflow staleness when run_id available - Graceful degradation when MLflow unavailable - Stall check frequency configurable (not every heartbeat cycle) - ERROR logged with actionable details when stall detected - All tests in make test-staging (mocked MLflow)

phase.title: Observability Contract Enforcement — Meta-Test Suite
phase.description:
Create a SINGLE comprehensive meta-test that enforces the observability contract across the entire codebase. This test runs at make test-staging and catches ANY violation of the contract when new code is added.
The observability contract (must be satisfied by EVERY flow): 1. GPU flows: import + call require_cuda_context() 2. GPU flows: import + use GpuHeartbeatMonitor as context manager 3. ALL flows: import + use StructuredLogger or flow_observability_context 4. ALL flows: call configure_flow_logging() 5. ALL @task: on_completion + on_failure hooks 6. ALL services in compose: HEALTHCHECK stanza
The meta-test: - Discovers all *_flow.py files in the flows/ directory - Classifies each as GPU or CPU based on a constant mapping - Runs all enforcement checks per classification - Reports ALL violations in a single test run (not fail-fast) - Prints a summary table: flow_name | cuda_guard | heartbeat | logging | hooks | compose_hc
This is the "guardrail" test — it prevents regression to the first-pass pattern of "wire it into train and forget the rest."

task.title: Create tests/v2/unit/orchestration/test_observability_contract.py
task.tdd-spec:
RED: The meta-test that: 1. Scans src/minivess/orchestration/flows/ for all *_flow.py files 2. For each file, parses AST and checks: - GPU flows: require_cuda_context import + call - GPU flows: GpuHeartbeatMonitor import + with statement - ALL flows: StructuredLogger or flow_observability_context import - ALL flows: configure_flow_logging call - ALL @task: on_completion + on_failure kwargs 3. Collects all violations without early exit 4. Reports violations as a formatted table in the assertion message 5. Fails if ANY violation exists
GPU flow list: ["train_flow.py", "hpo_flow.py", "post_training_flow.py", "analysis_flow.py"] CPU flow list: everything else that matches *_flow.py
Additionally: 6. Parses docker-compose.flows.yml and verifies each flow service has a healthcheck stanza (YAML parsing, not regex) 7. Cross-references the flow file list with the compose service list to ensure completeness
CRITICAL: The GPU flow list is defined as a CONSTANT in this test. When a new GPU flow is added, the developer MUST add it to this list or the test fails. This is intentional — it forces conscious acknowledgment of the observability contract.

phase.success-criteria: - Single test file enforces the COMPLETE observability contract - Any new flow file without observability wiring breaks the test - Any new @task without hooks breaks the test - Any compose service without healthcheck breaks the test - Violation report is human-readable (table format) - Test runs in make test-staging (<10s)

execution-order.note:
Phases can be executed in groups with clear dependencies:
GROUP 1 (Foundation — no dependencies): Phase 1: Observability helpers Phase 6: CPU healthcheck script Phase 8: .env.example update
GROUP 2 (GPU flow wiring — depends on Phase 1): Phase 2: CUDA guard for all GPU flows Phase 3: Heartbeat for all GPU flows Phase 10: Stall detection integration
GROUP 3 (All-flow wiring — depends on Phase 1): Phase 4: Structured logging for all flows Phase 5: Task hooks for all flows
GROUP 4 (Docker Compose — depends on Phases 6, 8): Phase 7: Compose healthchecks Phase 9: Grafana LGTM + OTEL compose
GROUP 5 (Enforcement — depends on all above): Phase 11: Meta-test enforcement suite
Recommended execution order: Phase 1 → Phase 8 → Phase 6 → Phase 2 → Phase 3 → Phase 10 → Phase 4 → Phase 5 → Phase 7 → Phase 9 → Phase 11
